content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcel PRACrrcEs CouurssroN
2128 J Street o Suire 620 r Sacramento, CA g5gl4_232g
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
February 9,2010
Kate J. Hart
2100 2ft Srreet
Sacramento, CA 95818
Dear Ms. Hart:
RE: Your Request for Assistance
Our File No. W-10-009
This letter is in response to your request for advice, on behalf of yourself regarding the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act',).1 ifr" e., requires the
Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question
or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 831 la@); Regulation 1g329(a), copy
enclosed.) However, the Commission may decline to provide written advice if it would be
inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation 1g32g(bxgxF).)
After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to
you at this time' Your current questioninvolvis an analysis of a series of interrelated decisions,
some of which have already occurred. Issuing advice atihis time would relate to past conduct
and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated
by section 831 I 1. Moreover, the Commission must decline to give advice at this time, as the
same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the
Enforcement Division
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
fr-/ M. (---
By: Raheel Hayat
Intern, Legal Division
' The Political Refbrm Act is contained in Government Code Sections g1000 through gl0l4. AII statutory
ret'erences are to the Government code' unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair political practices
commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through 18997 of ritle 2 of the california code of Regulations. All
regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the califbrnia Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

sections.conclusion: After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. Your current question involves an analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred. Issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 83111. Moreover, the Commission must decline to give advice at this time, as the same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor sought FPPC advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act applicable to their conduct.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the question involves analysis of past conduct and related interrelated decisions that would not further the Act's impartial administration. Additionally, substantially similar issues are currently under investigation by the Enforcement Division.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The question and conclusion were extracted from the body text. File number W-10-009 indicates a withdrawal/declination. Document signed by General Counsel with intern cosigner.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor sought FPPC advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act applicable to their conduct.

CONCLUSION: After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. Your current question involves an analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred. Issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 83111. Moreover, the Commission must decline to give advice at this time, as the same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide written advice to Kate J. Hart regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act, citing that the analysis involves past conduct and that substantially similar issues are currently the subject of a pending Enforcement Division investigation.
